Communication SystemsII.11 Compliance with GDC 19 Departure The NuScale design supports an exemption 
from GDC 19. As described in Section 3.1.2, the design complies with a NuScale-specific 
principal design criterion (PDC) in lieu of 
this GDC. Design documents meet requirements of PDC-19 for ensuring that 
communication equipment is provided at 
appropriate locations inside the control room with the capability to support all 
normal and emergency operations, 
including intra-plant communications and plant to emergency facilities and off-site 
communication requirements even in the 
event of a single failure within a communication subsystem or the loss of the 
normal power source. The design addresses 
control room communications so that control room can maintain communications 
with site and offsite entities during normal 
and accident conditions.9.5.2

nuscale_ch01_0402.summary: This section explains that the NuScale design departs from GDC 19 by supporting an exemption and instead complies with a NuScale-specific principal design criterion (PDC-19). The design ensures that communication systems in the control room can support all normal and emergency operations, including intra-plant and off-site communications, even during single failures or power loss. Additionally, certain regulatory requirements (10 CFR 73.45) are not applicable to NuScale since it does not reprocess spent fuel or handle special nuclear material.

nuscale_ch01_0119.title: 1.8.1 Combined License Information Items
nuscale_ch01_0119.content:
NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report Interfaces with Certified Design
Tier 2 1.8-1 Revision 51.8 Interfaces with Certified Design
This section addresses interface requirements  between the NuScale Power Plant certified 
design and the site-specific design provided in the combined licens e (COL) application. 
Section 1.2 identifies the structures, systems, an d components that are included in the certified 
design. Figure 1.2-1 provides a representation of  the overall facility and Figure 1.2-2 provides 
the general boundaries between the cert ified design and site-specific design.
Table 1.8-1 identifies the interfac es between the NuScale certifie d design and the site-specific 
design. There are two types of interface requirements described:
• CDI: Conceptual design information that is provided for the non-ce rtified portion of the 
plant to facilitate review of the certified desi gn and to confirm the adequacy of identified 
interface requirements. 
• COL: NuScale design assumptions related to site-specific design el ements that are the 
responsibility of the COL applicant. This type of interface is identified as a COL information 
item.
1.8.1 Combined License Information Items
Information that must be prov ided in order to li cense and operate a site-specific NuScale 
Power Plant, but is not included in the certif ied design, is identified  throughout the Final 
Safety Analysis Report as COL information items. Table 1.8-2 lists the COL information 
items, includes the COL info rmation item text and identifies the section where the 
information item is located. The COL applicant addresses each  COL information item in the 
section where it is located.
1.8.2 Departures
COL Item 1.8-1: A COL applicant that references th e NuScale Power Plant design certification will 
provide a list of departures from the certified design.

nuscale_ch01_0865.title: Nuclear Power Plants to be Operated on Multi-Unit Sites
nuscale_ch01_0865.content:
NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report Nuclear Power Plants to be Operated on Multi-Unit Sites
Tier 2 1.10-1 Revision 51.10 Nuclear Power Plants to be Operated on Multi-Unit Sites
COL Item 1.10-1: A COL applicant that references th e NuScale Power Plant design certification will 
evaluate the potential hazards resulting fr om construction activities of the new 
NuScale facility to the safety-related and risk significant structures, systems, and 
components of existing op erating unit(s) and newly co nstructed operating unit(s) 
at the co-located site per 10 CFR 52.79( a)(31). The evaluation will include 
identification of management and administ rative controls necessary to eliminate 
or mitigate the consequences of potent ial hazards and demonstration that the 
limiting conditions for operation of an op erating unit would not be exceeded. This 
COL item is not applicable for construction ac tivities (build-out of the facility) at an 
individual NuScale Power Plant with operating NuScale Power Modules.
nuscale_ch01_0865.summary: This section states that applicants referencing the NuScale Power Plant design certification must assess potential hazards from construction activities on multi-unit sites, ensuring the safety of both existing and new units. The evaluation should identify necessary management and administrative controls to mitigate risks and confirm that operational safety limits are not exceeded; however, this requirement does not apply to construction at a single NuScale plant with operating modules.

nuscale_ch01_0864.title: Nuclear Power Plants to be Operated on Multi-Unit Sites
nuscale_ch01_0864.content:
NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report Nuclear Power Plants to be Operated on Multi-Unit Sites
Tier 2 1.10-1 Revision 51.10 Nuclear Power Plants to be Operated on Multi-Unit Sites
COL Item 1.10-1: A COL applicant that references th e NuScale Power Plant design certification will 
evaluate the potential hazards resulting fr om construction activities of the new 
NuScale facility to the safety-related and risk significant structures, systems, and 
components of existing op erating unit(s) and newly co nstructed operating unit(s) 
at the co-located site per 10 CFR 52.79( a)(31). The evaluation will include 
identification of management and administ rative controls necessary to eliminate 
or mitigate the consequences of potent ial hazards and demonstration that the 
limiting conditions for operation of an op erating unit would not be exceeded. This 
COL item is not applicable for construction ac tivities (build-out of the facility) at an 
individual NuScale Power Plant with operating NuScale Power Modules.
nuscale_ch01_0864.summary: This section outlines requirements for applicants referencing the NuScale Power Plant design certification to assess and manage potential hazards from construction activities at multi-unit sites. The evaluation must ensure that safety-related structures and systems of both existing and new units are protected, and that operational safety limits are not exceeded. These requirements do not apply to construction activities at a single NuScale Power Plant with operating modules.

nuscale_ch01_0118.title: 1.8 Interfaces with Certified Design
nuscale_ch01_0118.content:
NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report Interfaces with Certified Design
Tier 2 1.8-1 Revision 51.8 Interfaces with Certified Design
This section addresses interface requirements  between the NuScale Power Plant certified 
design and the site-specific design provided in the combined licens e (COL) application. 
Section 1.2 identifies the structures, systems, an d components that are included in the certified 
design. Figure 1.2-1 provides a representation of  the overall facility and Figure 1.2-2 provides 
the general boundaries between the cert ified design and site-specific design.
Table 1.8-1 identifies the interfac es between the NuScale certifie d design and the site-specific 
design. There are two types of interface requirements described:
• CDI: Conceptual design information that is provided for the non-ce rtified portion of the 
plant to facilitate review of the certified desi gn and to confirm the adequacy of identified 
interface requirements. 
• COL: NuScale design assumptions related to site-specific design el ements that are the 
responsibility of the COL applicant. This type of interface is identified as a COL information 
item.
1.8.1 Combined License Information Items
Information that must be prov ided in order to li cense and operate a site-specific NuScale 
Power Plant, but is not included in the certif ied design, is identified  throughout the Final 
Safety Analysis Report as COL information items. Table 1.8-2 lists the COL information 
items, includes the COL info rmation item text and identifies the section where the 
information item is located. The COL applicant addresses each  COL information item in the 
section where it is located.
1.8.2 Departures
COL Item 1.8-1: A COL applicant that references th e NuScale Power Plant design certification will 
provide a list of departures from the certified design.

nuscale_ch01_0071.title: Interfaces with Certified Design
nuscale_ch01_0071.content:
NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report Interfaces with Certified Design
Tier 2 1.8-1 Revision 51.8 Interfaces with Certified Design
This section addresses interface requirements  between the NuScale Power Plant certified 
design and the site-specific design provided in the combined licens e (COL) application. 
Section 1.2 identifies the structures, systems, an d components that are included in the certified 
design. Figure 1.2-1 provides a representation of  the overall facility and Figure 1.2-2 provides 
the general boundaries between the cert ified design and site-specific design.
Table 1.8-1 identifies the interfac es between the NuScale certifie d design and the site-specific 
design. There are two types of interface requirements described:
• CDI: Conceptual design information that is provided for the non-ce rtified portion of the 
plant to facilitate review of the certified desi gn and to confirm the adequacy of identified 
interface requirements. 
• COL: NuScale design assumptions related to site-specific design el ements that are the 
responsibility of the COL applicant. This type of interface is identified as a COL information 
item.
1.8.1 Combined License Information Items
Information that must be prov ided in order to li cense and operate a site-specific NuScale 
Power Plant, but is not included in the certif ied design, is identified  throughout the Final 
Safety Analysis Report as COL information items. Table 1.8-2 lists the COL information 
items, includes the COL info rmation item text and identifies the section where the 
information item is located. The COL applicant addresses each  COL information item in the 
section where it is located.
1.8.2 Departures
COL Item 1.8-1: A COL applicant that references th e NuScale Power Plant design certification will 
provide a list of departures from the certified design.

nuscale_ch01_0071.summary: This section outlines the interface requirements between the NuScale Power Plant’s certified design and the site-specific design provided in the Combined License (COL) application. It describes the types of interface requirements (Conceptual Design Information and COL information items), identifies which structures, systems, and components are included in the certified design, and explains how site-specific information and departures from the certified design must be documented and addressed by the COL applicant.
